prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU? Well, from what I've gathered from your pa tinkering with those gadgets, it's like the brain of the whole computer, I suppose. It sits right inside there and tells everything else what to do—making the pictures move on the screen or helping you play those games you love. I don't quite get all the fancy wires and such, but it's the busy little boss that keeps the machine humming along without stopping. Isn't technology a wonder? Now, why don't you show me how it works on your tablet?
